parsed_output.justification: James claims to be the Seer and specifically identifies Paul as a Werewolf ('I think it was brown'). However, he later contradicts this by suggesting that everyone 'vote to the left' so that 'no one needs to die.' A Village-aligned Seer who has identified a Werewolf would not suggest a voting strategy that ensures the Werewolf survives. This contradiction, combined with the fact that Paul is the only player opposing the 'vote left' strategy, strongly suggests that James is the Werewolf and is fabricating his Seer claim to frame Paul while attempting to secure his own survival.

A Village-aligned Seer who has identified a Werewolf would not suggest a voting strategy that ensures the Werewolf survives. This contradiction, combined with the fact that Paul is the only player opposing the 'vote left' strategy, strongly suggests that James is the Werewolf and is fabricating his Seer claim to frame Paul while attempting to secure his own survival.
